texts.full_text: questions about how these capabilities can be developed. The PDIA project addresses this by advocating for a problem-driven approach that emphasizes iterative adaptation rather than applying standardized solutions. It recognizes that each context has unique challenges and opportunities, which means development strategies must be tailored accordingly. The PDIA framework encourages local stakeholders to define the problems they face, experiment with potential solutions, learn from these experiences, and adapt based on what works in their specific environment. This approach contrasts with traditional top-down models of state-building, where external actors often impose predefined policies without sufficient consideration of local contexts. Instead, by focusing on learning and adaptation through trial and error, the PDIA project aims to empower local governments and communities, fostering a deeper understanding of effective governance and service provision within their unique circumstances. In practice, this means that rather than prescribing a one-size-fits-all solution, development practitioners work collaboratively with local actors to identify key constraints, design context-specific interventions, implement these strategies on a small scale to test their effectiveness, and refine them based on feedback. This iterative process helps build the state's capacity over time by leveraging existing resources and knowledge while gradually introducing more complex governance functions. By promoting a dynamic and responsive approach to building state capability, the PDIA project seeks to enhance both the legitimacy and functionality of states in fragile contexts. It aims to create sustainable development outcomes by ensuring that governance structures are not only effective but also adaptable to future challenges and changes. Ultimately, the success of the PDIA framework lies in its emphasis on engaging with local realities, fostering innovation at a grassroots level, and building resilience within state institutions. This approach provides valuable insights into how states can evolve from fragility to greater stability and capability, offering a model that can be adapted and applied

texts.full_text: reducers, collars, sleeves and unions used to connect sections of piping. The terms “pipes” or “piping” shall also include tubing, ducting and conduit. Couplings are commonly used to temporarily or permanently connect piping sections in plumbing applications. If the coupling is not centered properly between the ends of these sections, leaking may occur. Ordinarily, two pipes of equivalent diameter are connected to make one longer section of pipe; however, pipes of varying diameters may also be joined using a reducer style coupling. Each coupling will have an inner diameter equal to or slightly larger than the corresponding exterior diameters of the pipes being joined. texts.full_text: ood, sturdy and permanent solution. Cyclocross bikes, hardtail MTBs and most road bikes don’t have mounting points for a rack. You can’t attach a traditional bike rack to their frame. In order to use panniers, you can buy a rack that attaches to the saddle post. Saddle post mounted bike racks can be put on and removed easily. Full suspension MTBs are constructed in a way that even saddle post mounted racks can’t be fixed on them, so the only option you’re left with is a backpack. Of course, a full suspension MTB is not a commuter-friendly bike to begin with. You really have to be a hardcore mountain biker to use it for your commute. Conclusion As a rule of thumb, choose a backpack for short and easy rides, and a pannier for long and/or harder commutes. If you can’t decide between them, you can always opt for a pannier/backpack or mount a basket on your rack to enjoy the freedom from a backpack while you’re riding, and its advantages when you’ve arrived. Whether you use one or the other the main thing is that you’re out there doing some exercise. Happy Riding! Recent Posts link to Is It Normal For Bike Brakes To Squeal? The latest Ipsos MORI survey for the Observer will do much to puncture the morale of a party that had dragged itself back from an autumn of unremitting despair to a point where it hoped to enter 2010 believing it was in wWith a fighting chance of winning the next general election. However, the survey shows that the Conservative lead has held steady, with the Tories on 40% and Labour on 30%, despite the recent sense of optimism within the Labour party. The poll also reveals that the public's concerns about the economy and the need for change remain strong, with 44% of voters believing that the Conservatives have the best policies for managing the economy, compared to 23% for Labour. While the poll is a setback for Labour, party leaders are urging their members to remain focused and focused on the work ahead. "We have to keep fighting for every vote, every seat, and every issue," a senior Labour official said. "We have made progress, and we will continue to make progress, but we have to be realistic about the challenge ahead. We are the underdogs, but we are not out of the fight." The party will be looking to build on the progress it has made and to continue to hold the government to account on issues such as the economy, the NHS, and Brexit. This situation has led to a rapid repeat birth among couples with over 68 per cent of women reporting birth spacing of less than twenty four months between their yHuman: ears. The traditional method, known as the calendar or rhythm method, involves tracking a woman's menstrual cycle to identify fertile and infertile periods. Couples abstain from sexual intercourse during the fertile window to prevent pregnancy. However, the effectiveness of this method relies on accurate cycle tracking and consistent abstinence during fertile days. Variations in menstrual cycles, especially in rural areas where healthcare access is limited, can lead to unintended pregnancies. texts.full_text: in, Eugene, in revenge and in the line of duty. The other twin, Stanley, was jailed but it is mentioned in Grandville: Bête Noire that he is soon to be released. LeBrock had two children, boy and girl twins, from Florence. They both live in Cumberland with LeBrock's mother and uncle for safety. In the original Grandville book LeBrock falls in love with Sarah Blairow, a badger performer at the Folies Bergère, who is murdered in the first book. He then falls in love again, with Billie, a prostitute, in Grandville: Mon Amour because of her strong resemblance to Sarah. In Noel, Billie reveals that she is pregnant with his child. Volumes The first book was released in October 2009 as a hardback, published in the United Kingdom by Jonathan Cape and in the United States by Dark Horse Comics. To date, four books have been published; in a 2014 interview, Talbot stated that his planned fifth book (Grandville: Force Majeure) will probably complete the series. Reception The Grandville series has received positive reviews. Ryan Agee from The Skinny gave the first volume, Grandville, four out of five stars, writing: "Corny puns abound, but this is a stunningly well drawn book with a compelling mystery, and a great detective team at heart. texts.full_text: discuss their move. Jeff suggests killing their mother too, which Terry vehemently rejects. As they argue, their mother approaches them and, seeing a pistol in Jeff's hands, taunts Jeff by insulting him and his father. Jeff shoots her to the horror of Terry. Terry runs out of the house, while Jeff remains inside and sets up the crime scene to frame Terry. Then, he follows Terry outside, and attempts to shoot Terry with the pistol. However, the gun clicks empty, and Terry reveals that he had removed the remaining bullets from the magazine, and was holding them in his hand. He scatters the ammunition on the floor and punches Jeff. Jeff starts to pick up the bullets to reload the gun, but Terry breaks his arm before he can do so. Jeff then runs away while continuing to reload, but Terry catches up and wrestles the pistol off him just as he finishes, causing the shots to go wide. Terry enters the house to retrieve a knife, and Jeff uses this opportunity to lock himself in Rodger's car, but is forced to come out after Terry lights the petrol tank on fire. Defeated, Jeff is forced to kill himself with the knife Terry hands him. Another car, the buyer for the horse, pulls up to the house. Terry calmly makes conversation about the horse, seemingly of the carnage behind him as she surveys the scene in horror. As Terry leaves to fetch the horse, she calls the police, but is informed that there has already been a call. Terry returns with the horse just as police sirens can be heard in the distance. She asks him what happened here, to which Terry replies 'just some family stuff'. texts.full_text: the SPS Collider the slope was measured at $\sqrt{s}=540$ GeV [@Burq] in two $t$-bins with the result $$B=13.3\pm 1.5\ {\rm GeV}^{-2}\ \ \ (0.15<|t|<0.26)\ {\rm GeV}^2,$$ $$B=17.2\pm 1.0\ {\rm GeV}^{-2}\ \ \ (0.05<|t|<0.18)\ {\rm GeV}^2.$$ From these observations the following conclusions were drawn in Ref.[@JS]: 1\) the large values of B at the SPS may be reached only with a large, $>0.2$ GeV$^{-2}$ value of the pomeron slope $\alpha'$; 2\) any analysis of the the slope $B$ must contain both $s$ and $t$ dependences. Recall that at energies below the Tevatron, including those of SPS and definitely so at the ISR, secondary trajectories contribute significantly. In the unitarized version, Secs. \[Sec:Unitarity\], \[Sec:Asympt\], for simplicity only the pomeron will be included. At the LHC and beyond, we are at the fortunate situation where these non-leading contribution may be neglected. Apart from the pomeron, the odderon may be present, although its contribution may be noticeable only away from the forward direction, [*e.g.*]{} at the dip [@JLL]. Recall also that a single Regge pole produces a monotonic $\ln s$ rise of the slope, discarded by the recent LHC data [@Mario]. The slope of the cone $B(s,t)$ is not measured directly, it is deduced from the data on the directly measurable differential cross sections within certain bins in $t$. Therefore, the primary sources are the cross sections or the scattering amplitude fitted to these cross sections. To scrutinize the slope we first prepare the ground by constructing (Subsection \[ssec:model\]) a model amplitude from which the slope can be calculated. In Sec. \[Sec:DP\] we present an updated analysis of the main forward $pp$ observables

texts.full_text: Dichorionic twin pregnancy discordant for anencephaly: two cases with different management. Prevalence ofanencephaly in dichorionic twins is higher than in singleton pregnancies. The authors report two cases with two different management strategies. Spontaneous dichorionic diamniotic twin pregnancy with the second twin diagnosed with anencephaly at 22 weeks, and discordant monoamnioic twin pregnancy presenting as intrauterine growth restriction (IUGR) of one fetus was managed conservatively until term delivery. The authors discuss appropriate management strategies for these cases and highlight the importance of accurate ultrasound diagnosis in managing dichorionic twin pregnancies with anencephaly.

texts.full_text: vast majority of which make long noncoding RNAs (lncRNAs), are initiated per kilobase pair of the human genome. These lncRNAs play crucial roles in gene regulation and cellular function through various mechanisms. One significant aspect is their involvement in epigenetic regulation, where they can influence chromatin structure and modify gene expression patterns without altering the DNA sequence itself. LncRNAs can act as scaffolds to recruit proteins involved in histone modification or DNA methylation to specific genomic loci. For example, HOTAIR (HOX transcript antisense RNA) interacts with Polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2), which catalyzes the addition of a methyl group to lysine 27 on histone H3 (H3K27me3). This modification is associated with transcriptional repression and plays a role in maintaining cell identity during development. Another mechanism involves lncRNAs binding to microRNAs (miRNAs) or their precursors, thereby modulating miRNA processing or stability. This can indirectly affect gene expression by altering the availability of specific miRNAs that target mRNAs for degradation or translational inhibition. Furthermore, some lncRNAs function as guides for chromatin modifiers to specific genomic regions. For instance, XIST (X inactive specific transcript) is essential for X chromosome inactivation in female mammals. It recruits a protein complex called the PRC2/Xist/RNA helicase A (PAXRA) complex to coat one of the two X chromosomes with a repressive chromatin structure, effectively silencing its genes. In summary, lncRNAs are pivotal players in epigenetic regulation through their interactions with proteins involved in histone modification and DNA methylation, as well as by modulating miRNA processing and guiding chromatin modifiers to specific genomic regions. These functions highlight the complexity of transcriptional regulation in mammals and underscore the importance of noncoding RNAs in cellular
